title: Huberantha keraudreniae
taxonomy: Huberantha keraudreniae is classified within the family Annonaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple or soursop family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its genetic relationships with other species in the Annonaceae family. Huberantha keraudreniae is further classified within the genus Huberantha, which is a genus of flowering plants that are native to Madagascar. The genus Huberantha is part of the larger family Annonaceae and is closely related to other genera in the same family. Huberantha keraudreniae is a member of the plant kingdom, specifically belonging to the kingdom Plantae, which consists of a vast array of plant species that are characterized by their ability to photosynthesize.

intro: Huberantha keraudreniae, also known as the Keraudren's huberantha, is a flowering plant species in the family Annonaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Madagascar, where it is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Huberantha keraudreniae include lignans, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as in the form of dried leaves, roots, or bark, to promote overall well-being and treat various health issues.
